title: Interactive Shiny Application
text: The centrepiece of this project is an interactive R Shiny dashboard that turns our analysis into an exploratory investigation tool. Filter the data, follow the timeline, trace the communication network, and weigh the evidence for each competing hypothesis.

title: Interactive Shiny Application
text:
Live application
The app is deployed on shinyapps.io. If the frame below is blank, the instance may be spinning up from sleep — give it a few seconds, or open it directly using the button above.


  wujiayan.shinyapps.io/VAST_Challenge_2026

title: Interactive Shiny Application
text:
What’s inside
The dashboard is organised into five tabs, mirroring the project’s analytical objectives:



🗂️


Overview

Orientation to the case, the dataset, and the two competing hypotheses that frame the investigation.





⏱️


Timeline & Leak

The minute-by-minute breach window (4:00–6:00 PM, 5 June), highlighting the social-media leak and the embargo lift.





🕸️


Agent & Communication Flow

The communication network between the seven AI agents, with centrality metrics to surface key coordinators and bottlenecks.





⚖️


Evidence & Judgment

A competing-hypotheses board that systematically weighs evidence for a deliberate leak versus a system breakdown.





📋


Summary & Conclusions

The investigative verdict, consolidated findings across all three tasks, the competing-hypotheses assessment, and recommended future work.

title: Interactive Shiny Application
text:
Built with
The application is written in R and assembled from the project’s task modules (task1.R, task2.R, task3.R) and a summary_tab.R module into a single combined_app.R. Key packages include:

Interactive dashboard — shiny, shinydashboard, bslib, DT
Network analysis — tidygraph, igraph, ggraph, visNetwork
Text analysis — tidytext, quanteda, wordcloud
Visualisation — ggplot2, plotly, lubridate, tidyverse

title: Interactive Shiny Application
text:
Run it locally
# Clone the repository, then from the "Shiny app" folder:
install.packages(c(
  "shiny", "shinydashboard", "bslib", "DT", "tidyverse",
  "tidygraph", "igraph", "ggraph", "visNetwork",
  "tidytext", "plotly", "lubridate"
))

shiny::runApp("combined_app.R")

title: Investigation of Merger Information Leak
text:
VAST CHALLENGE 2026 Mini-Challenge 1




Did TenantThread’s AI agents deliberately leak the merger — or did the compliance system simply break down under pressure?


On 5 June 2046, confidential merger information between TenantThread and CivicLoom Realty Partners surfaced online at 5:00 PM — exactly one hour before the official embargo lift. We use network analysis, text mining, and interactive visual analytics across 912 internal communications to reconstruct what really happened.


A project by BUI Tien Thanh, HU Yue & WU Jiayan · ISSS608 Visual Analytics & Applications


🚀 Launch Shiny App 📄 Read the Proposal 🖼️ View the Poster

title: Investigation of Merger Information Leak
section: What we set out to do
text:
What we set out to do
The investigation is organised around four analytical objectives, each surfaced as an interactive lens inside the Shiny application:

Reconstruct the event sequence during the breach window (4:00–6:00 PM, 5 June) using network analysis and timeline visualisation.
Analyse behavioural patterns by comparing breach-day behaviour against a baseline to detect anomalies consistent with deliberate action or system strain.
Identify key actors and information flow through centrality analysis to find critical coordinators and bottlenecks.
Apply a competing-hypotheses framework to systematically weigh evidence for a deliberate leak versus a system breakdown.

title: Investigation of Merger Information Leak

Singapore Management University · Master of IT in Business · ISSS608 · 2026

title: Group Project Proposal: VAST Challenge 2026 MC1
text:
On June 5th, 2046, confidential merger information between TenantThread and CivicLoom Realty Partners (codenamed “Project HarborCrest”) was leaked on social media at 5:00 PM—exactly one hour before the official 6:00 PM embargo lift. This premature disclosure triggered an investigation into whether the leak was deliberate sabotage or an organizational failure under extreme pressure.
TenantThread operated with 7 AI agents managing communications, compliance, and social media during the critical two-week period leading to the announcement. The breach raises fundamental questions about AI-driven organizational systems: when they fail, is it by design or by overload?
Central Investigative Question: Did TenantThread’s team deliberately leak the merger information, or did the compliance system simply break down under pressure?
This project will use visual analytics, network analysis, and text mining to examine 912 internal communications across 14 days to distinguish between these competing explanations.

title: Group Project Proposal: VAST Challenge 2026 MC1
text:
4.1 Dataset Description

Source: VAST Challenge 2026 Mini-Challenge 1 (MC1)
File: MC1_final.json
Format: Nested JSON structure
Size: 912 communications across 23 time-stamped rounds
Agents: 7 AI agents (Legal, PR, Social Media, Quality, Judge, PR Intern, Intern)
Time Period: May 23, 2046 - June 5, 2046 (14 days)
Critical Window: June 5, 2046, 4:00 PM - 6:00 PM

title: Group Project Proposal: VAST Challenge 2026 MC1
text:
4.2 Key Data Components








Component
Description
Records




communications
Agent messages with metadata
912 messages


environment_context
External events and media pressure
23 rounds


internal_state
Agent private reasoning (sparse)
86 records


rounds
Time-stamped system snapshots
23 rounds

title: Group Project Proposal: VAST Challenge 2026 MC1
text:
4.3 Important Variables
Network Construction: - agent_id: Agent identifier for nodes - responding_to: Reply reference for building edges - channel: Communication channel (comms_huddle, one_on_one_chat, side_huddle, etc.)
Text Analysis: - message_text: Full message content - internal_state: Private agent reasoning (reacting, rationalizing, deliberating)
Temporal Analysis: - timestamp: Message datetime - round_hour: Round time marker
Context: - environment_context: External events, media pressure, critical deadlines - message_type: Type of communication

title: Group Project Proposal: VAST Challenge 2026 MC1
text:
6.1 Shiny App Design
The proposed Shiny app is designed as an interactive dashboard for investigating whether the TenantThread merger exposure was a deliberate leak or a system-control failure. The prototype contains four main tabs:








Figure 2: Low-fidelity hand-drawn Shiny App prototype sketch.






6.1.1 Tab 1: Overview

Provides a high-level summary of the crisis.
Shows key indicators such as total messages, merger-related messages, leak/exposure signals, and pre-embargo public exposure.
Presents the final interpretation: internal confidentiality leak enabled by system-level control failure.



6.1.2 Tab 2: Timeline & Slack Leak

Focuses on how the crisis developed over time.
Displays communication volume, pressure signal heatmap, and the Slack screenshot escalation timeline.
Highlights the turning point from market inference to direct internal leakage.



6.1.3 Tab 3: Agent & Communication Flow

Shows which agents were most involved in the communication and pressure patterns.
Includes agent-level pressure exposure and communication-flow visuals.
Helps users understand why the system failed to contain sensitive information.



6.1.4 Tab 4: Evidence & Judgment

Summarises the final evidence used to classify the incident.
Displays the evidence interpretation matrix comparing internal leak, system breakdown, and senior-orchestrated leak.
Provides the final judgment that the incident was an internal leak amplified by weak multi-agent controls, not a proven senior-level deliberate leak.

title: Minutes of Meeting
text: This page documents the three project meetings held by the team, tracing our path from initial analysis through alignment on the final conclusion to the delegation of tasks. The original Word documents are available for download below.

title: Minutes of Meeting
text:
Meeting 1 — Project Kick-off & Assignment Review



1



Visual Analytics Project Discussion 1


📅 27 May 2026🕚 11:04 AM · 27 min💻 Zoom Online👥 Thanh · Yue · Jiayan



1. Take-Home Assignment 2 Discussion

Overview — BUI Tien Thanh reviewed the requirements for Mini-Challenge 1: a network-graph dataset representing a networking data center that the team must analyse and support with visual evidence (Question 3 in particular requires visuals/proof).
Challenges — Members noted that working with a network dataset is difficult, as it is a new data format for the team.
Analysis & Findings

WU Jiayan shared an early analysis suggesting the leak was deliberate and intentional rather than a system failure, based on a timeline of social-media posts and senior–junior agent interactions leading up to the 5 June announcement.
BUI Tien Thanh and HU Yue noted conflicting preliminary findings on whether the incident was a breakdown or an intentional leak, agreeing the results needed further alignment.

Learning Resources — Review Lesson 9 (“Visual Analytics for Network Data”); text analysis of the conversations will be critical.
Professor Consultation — Book a consultation if the team hits persistent roadblocks, bringing specific, well-thought-out questions.

2. Group Project Planning

Scope — Build an interactive Shiny application from the Assignment 2 analysis, allowing users to filter elements.
Objectives — Primarily an academic demonstration of Shiny and analytical skills.
Work Distribution — Rather than picking tasks immediately, all members complete Assignment 2 individually, then assign project responsibilities based on who executed each section best.
Timeline — Assignment 2 due Sunday 7 June; BUI Tien Thanh to set up a shared GitHub repository.

3. Action Items








Action Item
Responsible
Deadline




Complete Take-Home Assignment 2
All members
Ongoing / 7 June


Set up shared GitHub repository
BUI Tien Thanh
Before next meeting


Schedule next alignment meeting
All members
Around the A2 deadline

title: Minutes of Meeting
text:
Meeting 2 — Conclusion Alignment & Task Delegation



2



Visual Analytics Project Discussion 2


📅 6 June 2026🕒 3:50 PM · 60 min📍 LKCSB GSR 1-1👥 Thanh · Yue · Jiayan



1. Project Case Conclusion Alignment
The team aligned on the final conclusion for the project.

Initial perspectives

BUI Tien Thanh — leaned toward a system breakdown under pressure, citing suspicious vocabulary shifts and the “judge agent” failing repeatedly.
HU Yue — proposed a combination: an intentional leak enabled by a control failure, followed by deliberate crisis management.
WU Jiayan — early events (e.g. the 29 May social-media message) were unintentional system failures per the agents’ internal-state logs, but the breach-day (5 June) actions were deliberate — agents purposefully excluded the newly deployed judge agent from side huddles to stage an early announcement.

Agreed conclusion — Events before 4:00 PM on 5 June were unintentional system failures; actions after 4:00 PM, in response to the Southwind announcement, were deliberate and intentional.

2. Proposal & Task Delegation

Deadline — Officially Wednesday 10 June, buffer until Friday; team aims for Friday at the latest.
Allocation — WU Jiayan: Methodology (diagrams & explanations) · BUI Tien Thanh: Project Schedule (Gantt) · HU Yue: Prototype / hand sketch.
Shiny app structure — A dashboard with multiple tabs and sub-tabs presenting the different tasks and visualisations.

3. Repository & Version Control

GitHub repository set up by BUI Tien Thanh.
Workflow via RStudio / VS Code terminals (git clone, pull, push).
To avoid merge conflicts, members notify the group on WeChat before editing/pushing proposal.qmd.
